name: New Zealand
overview: New Zealand's Privacy Act 2020 replaced the 1993 Act, adding mandatory breach notification, compliance notices, extraterritorial application and cross-border disclosure safeguards under 13 Information Privacy Principles (IPPs). New Zealand holds EU adequacy. The Privacy Amendment Act 2025 added IPP 3A requiring notification when personal information is collected indirectly.

legal_instruments.title: Privacy Act 2020
legal_instruments.summary: Principles-based law (13 IPPs) covering agencies public and private, with notifiable privacy breach regime, Commissioner compliance notices, access directions, and IPP 12 rules for overseas disclosure.

legal_instruments.title: Privacy Amendment Act 2025
legal_instruments.summary: Adds IPP 3A: agencies collecting personal information from sources other than the individual must take reasonable steps to notify the individual (effective 1 May 2026).

developments.title: Privacy Amendment Act 2025 receives Royal Assent
developments.summary: Parliament enacted the indirect-collection notification requirement (IPP 3A), applying to information collected from 1 May 2026.

developments.title: Biometrics Code consultation
developments.summary: The Privacy Commissioner consulted on a Biometric Processing Privacy Code regulating facial recognition and other biometric uses.

penalties: Modest criminal fines (up to NZD 10,000 for offences such as non-compliance with access directions); no general administrative fine regime. Human Rights Review Tribunal damages available.
cross_border: IPP 12: disclosure overseas only if the recipient is subject to comparable safeguards (law, binding scheme, contract) or the individual authorises the disclosure after being informed.
